text.title: Pricing Experiment Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pricing Experiment Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Existing customers grandfathered at current pricing unless they explicitly upgrade. Surprise price increases churn faster than they earn.
rule.rationale: SaaS churn studies: silent re-pricing of existing customers = 2-4× churn spike + brand damage.

rule.statement: A/B by visitor cohort (geo, traffic source, time-of-day), NEVER by identifiable individual. Individual price discrimination crosses ethical + sometimes legal lines.
rule.rationale: EU + several US states prohibit personalized pricing without disclosure; cohort segmentation is the safe ceiling.

rule.statement: Compute MDE before launching. Common SaaS test: MDE 10-20% on conversion requires 1-3k visitors per arm.
rule.rationale: Underpowered tests waste 4-8 weeks; pre-launch MDE math prevents.

rule.statement: Use van Westendorp pricing survey to identify ranges BEFORE testing. Survey reveals 'too cheap' and 'too expensive' anchors.
rule.rationale: Live tests outside van-Westendorp range waste traffic; pre-survey narrows the test range.

rule.statement: Pre-defined roll-back trigger: revenue drop > 10% over 14 days. Don't 'give it time' on revenue regression.
rule.rationale: Pricing regressions compound; explicit rollback prevents quarter-scale damage.
